flashcards.back: A state where the system spends more time swapping pages than executing processes, leading to near-zero effective CPU utilization.
The set of memory pages a process actively uses within a specific time frame, reflecting its locality of reference.
Insufficient memory to hold the working sets of all active processes, leading to excessive page faults and disk I/O.

flashcards.front: How does locality of reference relate to thrashing?
flashcards.back: Processes tend to access specific memory regions (locality), but without enough memory, they constantly page-fault, triggering thrashing.
Increase physical memory, use the working set model for page replacement, or limit the degree of multiprogramming.
